name: Beef Balls (Thit Bo Vien)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup plus 1 tablespoon nuoc mam (Vietnamese fish sauce)
2 lbs trimmed boneless beef hind shank
recipeInstructions:
This recipe is inspired by a Vietnamese meatball soup you may have tried in a restaurant. The meatballs are often served as an appetizer or added to noodle soups. Chili sauce (tuong ot) is the usual accompaniment.
[Tuong ot is a paste made by smashing hot red chilies and garlic. Philippine "Sambal Oelek" or Thai Sriracha are good substitutes.]
1. In a shallow dish, mix together the fish sauce, potato starch, baking powder, sugar, and black pepper.
2. Slice the beef into 1/8-inch-thick pieces. Add to the marinade and mix well. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
3. Before proceeding, transfer the meat to the freezer for 30 minutes. Work with half of the beef at a time; do not overload the work bowl.
4. In a food processor, combine half of the beef with half of the garlic and sesame oil. Process until completely smooth but stiff, about 3 minutes. Stop occasionally to scrape down the sides of the work bowl. The finished paste should spring back when touched.
5. Transfer the paste to a bowl. Process the remaining beef, garlic, and sesame oil the same way.
6. Rub some vegetable oil on one hand. Grab a handful of the meat paste and close your hand into a fist, squeezing out a small portion of the mixture between your thumb and index finger. Keep rolling and squeezing the same portion until you have a smooth rounded ball. Scoop out the meatball with an oiled spoon. Repeat until all of the paste is used.
7. Pour 1 inch of water into a wok or wide pot. Place a steamer rack or bamboo steamer over the water. Arrange the meatballs without crowding in a single layer on the rack. Cover and steam for 5 minutes.
8. Serve as an appetizer with chili sauce. The beef balls can also be added to a well-seasoned beef broth, sprinkled with chopped scallions and black pepper, and served as a soup (noodles may be added).
Yield: about 60 meatballs
